comments.comment: Reported nitrogen oxides emissions are calculated from point source stationary diesel combustion from various generators and equipment on site. Emissions are estimated using Environment Canada Diesel Fuel Generator - Fuel Usage.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
A calculation error was found in spreadsheet, resulting in drilled TPM emissions to be reported in Kg oppose to tonnes. 

Reported fugitive emissions of Total Particulate Matter, PM10, and PM2.5  are cumulative emissions from stationary diesel generator combustion, unpaved road dust from mining pit and forest service road access and blasting material. 

Point source stationary diesel combustion fugitive emissions are estimated  using Environment Canada Diesel Fuel Generator.

Road dust emissions from mining pit and forest service road access is estimated using Environment Canada Unpaved Industrial Road Dust Calculator.

Fugitive emissions from blasting are estimated using calculations from EPA AP 42, fifth Edition, Volume 1; Chapter 11: Mineral Productions Industry; Section 11.9 Western Surface Coal Mining; Table 11.9-2 Emission Factor Equations for Uncontrolled Open Dust Sources at Western Surface Coal Mines.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
In 2024, Conuma Resources refined its Greenhouse Gas (GHG) reporting process by improving the allocation of diesel fuel consumption across its primary emission sources: General Stationary Combustion and Mobile Combustion. This refinement resulted in a greater proportion of diesel fuel being attributed to Mobile Combustion, and a corresponding reduction in diesel allocated to General Stationary Combustion.

Because diesel fuel use under General Stationary Combustion is used to calculate a portion of NPRI Part 4 substance emissions (using Diesel_Fuel_Generator-Fuel_Usage_2022_09_28.xlsx), this reallocation led to a noticeable decrease in reported Stack or Point Release values for nitrogen oxides, sulphur dioxide and carbon monoxide in 2024. The decrease in reported values reflects more accurate fuel attribution and improved alignment between operational fuel use and emissions reporting categories.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
In 2024, Conuma Resources refined its Greenhouse Gas (GHG) reporting process by improving the allocation of diesel fuel consumption across its primary emission sources: General Stationary Combustion and Mobile Combustion. This refinement resulted in a greater proportion of diesel fuel being attributed to Mobile Combustion, and a corresponding reduction in diesel allocated to General Stationary Combustion.

Because diesel fuel use under General Stationary Combustion is used to calculate a portion of NPRI Part 4 substance emissions (using Diesel_Fuel_Generator-Fuel_Usage_2022_09_28.xlsx), this reallocation led to a noticeable decrease in reported Stack or Point Release values for nitrogen oxides, sulphur dioxide and carbon monoxide in 2024. The decrease in reported values reflects more accurate fuel attribution and improved alignment between operational fuel use and emissions reporting categories.
